questions.q: What is built by combining the DOM and CSSOM?
What does V8 hidden class optimization help with?
questions.a: Fast property access on objects with stable shapes

questions.q: When do microtasks run in the event loop?
questions.a: After the current task finishes and before the next macrotask
The time until the largest visible content element is rendered

questions.q: Which resource hint requests an asset with high priority for the current page?
Which cache strategy returns cached content immediately and refreshes in the background?

questions.a: They stop HTML parsing until the script is fetched and executed.

questions.q: Why are CSS transforms cheaper than changing layout-affecting properties?
questions.a: Transforms usually avoid layout and paint work and can be handled by compositing.

questions.q: Why is HTTP/2 better than HTTP/1.1 for many small assets?
questions.a: It multiplexes requests over one connection and reduces connection overhead.

questions.q: What happens when TurboFan sees a type assumption violation?
questions.a: It deoptimizes and falls back to a less specialized code path.

questions.q: What is the role of a service worker?
questions.a: It intercepts network requests and can provide caching, offline behavior, and background work.

questions.q: Why does cache validation with ETag help?
questions.a: It avoids refetching unchanged content while still confirming freshness.

questions.q: Why does splitting code improve initial page load?
questions.a: It lets the browser download only the code needed for the first render.

questions.q: Why can microtask starvation hurt rendering?
questions.a: A long chain of microtasks can delay the browser from returning to rendering and input handling.

questions.q: Why does connection pooling help page loads?
questions.a: It reduces handshake overhead and reuses warmed connections for subresource fetches.

questions.q: Why are critical CSS and inline styles useful for first paint?
questions.a: They let the browser render above-the-fold content without waiting for large stylesheet downloads.

questions.q: Why are service workers powerful but risky?
questions.a: They can fully control fetches and caches, so bugs can trap users behind stale assets.

questions.q: Why does browser performance work often focus on the critical path rather than total bytes?
questions.a: The slowest render-blocking work determines when the user sees useful content.
